# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.128.0/http/server.ts";
import { createKvStore } from "./kv_store.ts";

// KV store for the edge database
const kv = createKvStore('my_app');

interface User {
  userId: string;
  filterParam?: any; // custom filter parameter
}

serve((req) => {
  if (req.method !== 'GET') {
    return new Response('Method not allowed.', { status: 405 });
  }

  const userQuery = await req.json();

  if (!userQuery || !(userQuery instanceof Array)) {
    return new Response('Invalid request body.', { status: 400 });
  }

  let results: User[] = [];

  for (const user of userQuery) {
    const key = ['users', user.userId, user.filterParam || '']; // construct key with optional filter parameter

    try {
      const value = await kv.get(key);
      if (value !== null) {
        results.push({ userId: user.userId, ...JSON.parse(value) });
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(`Error accessing KV key: ${key}, error: ${err}`);
    }
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(results), { status: 200 });
})

async function createKvStore(storeName: string) {
  const kvStore = await Deno.openKv({ name: storeName });
  // You can initialize the KV store with some data here if needed
  return kvStore;
}
